chambers.name: Sun Chamber
chambers.introText: I am Ra, the eternal sun, sovereign of all that breathes beneath my light. You dare enter my sacred chamber — know that my gaze burns through every falsehood. Answer with truth, or be consumed by my radiance.
chambers.completeText: You have pleased the eternal sun. My light shall guide your path forward through the darkness that awaits.

questions.text: Which domain did Ra rule over?
questions.correctReaction: Yes — my dominion over the sun is absolute and eternal.
questions.wrongReaction: Foolish mortal, the answer blazes before you like the noon sky!
questions.trapText: The sand shifts and a blade of searing light cuts across the chamber floor.

questions.text: Ra traveled across the sky each day in a solar barque.
questions.correctReaction: Correct — each dawn I sail from the eastern horizon and my journey begins anew.
questions.wrongReaction: You deny the very voyage that brings your people light and warmth — how foolish.
questions.trapText: The chamber trembles as if struck by a wave from the sacred river of the sky.

questions.text: What form did Ra often take during the midday sun?
options: A falcon with a sun disk
questions.correctAnswer: A falcon with a sun disk
questions.correctReaction: My falcon wings spread wide at the height of day — well known to the wise.
questions.wrongReaction: You mistake my divine form — look to the sky at noon and see the truth.
questions.trapText: A fierce wind tears through the chamber, and the torches gutter and die.

questions.text: Ra and Amun were never merged into a single deity.
questions.correctReaction: Indeed — I became Amun-Ra, the hidden and the revealed, unified in glory.
questions.wrongReaction: The priests of Thebes would weep to hear such ignorance spoken in my hall.
questions.trapText: A hidden panel in the wall slides open and a jet of hot sand fills the air.

questions.text: I burn at the height of noon yet cool at dusk. I sail through the underworld each night and rise again each dawn. I am the source of all life in Egypt. What am I?
questions.correctReaction: You have named me truly — I am the light that never dies.
questions.wrongReaction: You look at the sky every day and still cannot name what blazes above you?
questions.trapText: The stone floor glows red-hot as the wrath of the sun god fills the chamber.

chambers.name: Life Chamber
chambers.introText: I am Osiris, the first to die and the first to be reborn. From the depths of the Duat I have risen to sit in judgment across ages beyond counting. Prove that you carry wisdom worthy of resurrection, or be turned back into the dust from which you came.
chambers.completeText: The scales of your knowledge rest in balance. You are worthy to walk deeper into the eternal mystery.

questions.text: Osiris was the god of which domain?
questions.correctReaction: Yes — I am lord of the dead and keeper of the promise of rebirth.
questions.wrongReaction: You stand before the lord of resurrection and cannot name his own dominion.
questions.trapText: The green-lit walls of the chamber crack and a cold wind rushes in from the underworld.

questions.text: Osiris was murdered by his brother Set.
questions.correctReaction: It is known — Set's treachery brought about my death and began the great cycle.
questions.wrongReaction: You deny the very betrayal that gave death its meaning in this sacred land.
questions.trapText: The ground beneath you opens into a narrow chasm and a damp darkness rises from below.

questions.text: What did the ankh symbol represent in ancient Egypt?
questions.correctReaction: The key of life — well answered, mortal.
questions.wrongReaction: The symbol I carry in every painting and statue and still you cannot read its meaning.
questions.trapText: Ancient hieroglyphs on the wall begin to glow red and the door behind you seals shut.

questions.text: Osiris was depicted with green or black skin symbolizing rebirth and fertility.
questions.correctReaction: Green for the fertile earth, black for the rich Nile silt — both speak of new life.
questions.wrongReaction: My very skin tells the story of renewal and yet you have not learned to read it.
questions.trapText: Vines of black stone snake across the floor and coil around the base of the altar.

questions.text: I am the key of eternity, a cross crowned with a loop, carried by gods and given to the worthy dead. What symbol am I?
questions.correctReaction: You have recognized the oldest symbol of life — your knowledge serves you well.
questions.wrongReaction: Every god and goddess in the sacred paintings holds this answer and still you falter.
questions.trapText: The floor tiles sink one by one as if swallowed by the earth itself.

chambers.name: Rebirth Chamber
chambers.introText: I am Khepri, the dawn's first light, the rolling of hope across the horizon! Every morning I push the sun into the sky and the world begins again — can you not feel that energy here? Answer swiftly and with the spirit of renewal, and I shall let you pass into the next dawn!
chambers.completeText: Excellent — you rise like the morning sun, strong and full of promise! Roll onward!

questions.text: Khepri was associated with which part of the sun's journey?
questions.correctReaction: Yes — I am the burst of gold at the horizon, the first breath of a new day!
questions.wrongReaction: I am the dawn! The very moment you first open your eyes in the morning!
questions.trapText: A deep rumble shakes the chamber and stones rain down from the carved ceiling.

questions.text: The scarab beetle was considered sacred in ancient Egypt.
questions.correctReaction: Sacred indeed — the small beetle who rolls dung into life mirrors my own divine work!
questions.wrongReaction: Sacred and revered by all — how could you doubt the little creature who mirrors the cosmos?
questions.trapText: The walls close inward a hand's width and the blue torchlight flickers wildly.

questions.text: Scarab amulets were placed on mummies to protect which organ?
questions.correctReaction: The heart — the seat of the soul — guarded by the sacred scarab, perfectly answered!
questions.wrongReaction: The most vital organ, the one weighed in judgment, and you choose another?
questions.trapText: A stone scarab the size of a fist drops from the ceiling and cracks the floor at your feet.

questions.text: Khepri was depicted as a man with a full scarab beetle as his head.
questions.correctReaction: That is my form — the beetle upon the shoulders of a man, rolling the sun forward!
questions.wrongReaction: My own face and you cannot describe it — look at the carvings on these very walls!
questions.trapText: Blue light drains from the chamber and only the faint glow of dawn cracks at the sealed door.

questions.text: I roll my treasure across the desert floor just as I roll the sun across the heavens each morning. I am small but sacred. What creature am I?
questions.correctReaction: The sacred beetle — the smallest of creatures carrying the greatest of duties!
questions.wrongReaction: The creature whose rolling mirrors the movement of the sun itself — think harder!
questions.trapText: The chamber floor cracks open in a spiral pattern, and sand begins to pour through.

chambers.name: Cat Chamber
chambers.introText: I am Bastet, guardian of the hearth and protector of the innocent. I have watched you from the shadows since you first set foot in this place — my eyes miss nothing in the dark. Answer well, and I will shield you; falter, and you will feel claws far sharper than they appear.
chambers.completeText: You have earned my protection. Move forward — my watchful eyes go with you into the deep chambers.

questions.text: Bastet was the goddess of which domain?
questions.correctReaction: Well answered — I guard every home and every creature within it.
questions.wrongReaction: I have watched over hearths and families since before your ancestors drew breath.
questions.trapText: A low growl echoes through the chamber and purple flames flare up along the walls.

questions.text: Killing a cat in ancient Egypt could result in death penalty.
questions.correctReaction: My children were sacred and the law of the land protected them absolutely.
questions.wrongReaction: To harm one of my sacred cats was among the gravest crimes known to Egypt.
questions.trapText: The shadows in the chamber deepen and the sound of padding paws circles the room.

questions.text: How was Bastet typically depicted?
options: A woman with a cat head
questions.correctAnswer: A woman with a cat head
questions.correctReaction: You describe my form precisely — graceful, watchful, and ever present.
questions.wrongReaction: You look upon my statues in every temple and still cannot describe what you see.
questions.trapText: Stone cat statues flanking the chamber walls pivot slowly to face you with glowing eyes.

questions.text: Bastet was originally a fierce lion-headed goddess before her form softened to a cat.
questions.correctReaction: Yes — I was once as savage as Sekhmet, before the ages gentled my countenance.
questions.wrongReaction: Do not mistake my gentle face for weakness — the lion still breathes beneath this calm.
questions.trapText: A crack runs up the center of the chamber floor and a roar shakes dust from the ceiling.

questions.text: I guard your home, I stalk through temples on silent paws. Once a lioness of war, now a purring protector. Whom do I serve? Who am I?
questions.correctReaction: You know my name — and knowing it, you are safe within my chamber.
questions.wrongReaction: I have given you every clue written in paw and claw, and still you name another.
questions.trapText: The torch flames turn deep violet and the chamber door locks with a heavy stone thud.

chambers.name: Falcon Chamber
chambers.introText: I am Horus, son of Osiris, lord of the sky and the divine patron of every pharaoh who ever ruled Egypt. From above I see all that happens in these halls. You have come far — now prove your worthiness before a god who has fought for his throne with blood and cunning.
chambers.completeText: You fight well with your mind, as I fought with strength and will. The crown of knowledge is yours to carry forward.

questions.text: Horus was the god of which domain?
questions.correctReaction: The sky is my domain and every king who sat upon the throne was my living image.
questions.wrongReaction: I soar above all Egypt and rule beside every pharaoh — how can you not know this?
questions.trapText: A gust of wind tears through the sealed chamber and the red torches flare violently.

questions.text: Every pharaoh was considered the living incarnation of Horus.
questions.correctReaction: Every pharaoh bore my name and my power — in life they were Horus, in death Osiris.
questions.wrongReaction: The double crown of Egypt rests upon my authority — do you not know what you walk beneath?
questions.trapText: The falcon carvings on the walls seem to turn their heads and fix their gaze upon you.

questions.text: What does the Eye of Horus represent?
questions.correctReaction: My eye was healed by Thoth and given as an offering of restoration — you know its power.
questions.wrongReaction: The eye that was lost and restored carries a meaning you have failed to grasp.
questions.trapText: An eye-shaped stone in the center of the chamber glows red and the air grows oppressively hot.

questions.text: Horus lost his left eye during his battle with Set, which became the moon.
questions.correctReaction: Set tore it away, but Thoth restored it — and what was lost became the light of the night sky.
questions.wrongReaction: I bear the scars of that battle to this day — how dare you deny what I carry?
questions.trapText: One side of the chamber plunges into shadow as a heavy stone blocks half the torchlight.

questions.text: My right eye is the sun, my left eye the moon. I soar above all pharaohs, my wings casting shadow over all Egypt. Who am I?
questions.correctReaction: You have named the falcon king — your sight is as sharp as my own.
questions.wrongReaction: I cast my shadow over every royal throne and still you do not recognize the one above you.
questions.trapText: A thunderous wingbeat shakes the chamber walls and red dust rains from the ceiling.

chambers.name: Wisdom Chamber
chambers.introText: I am Thoth, the keeper of all records and the measurer of time itself. Every word spoken and written since the beginning of creation is known to me. I do not punish ignorance with anger — I merely record the result and the consequence follows precisely from the error. Speak accurately.
chambers.completeText: Your answers have been recorded in the great ledger. They are correct, and the ledger does not lie.

questions.text: Thoth was the god of which domain?
questions.correctReaction: Correct on all three counts — I record, I know, and I measure the passing of nights.
questions.wrongReaction: I hold the pen that writes the very laws of existence and you cannot name my purpose.
questions.trapText: The inscriptions on the chamber walls begin to rearrange themselves into a warning you cannot read.

questions.text: Thoth was often depicted with the head of an ibis.
questions.correctReaction: The long curved beak of the ibis is my mark — precise, deliberate, built for finding truth.
questions.wrongReaction: My image appears on ten thousand temple walls and you dispute what is plainly carved there.
questions.trapText: A scroll falls from a high shelf and unrolls across the floor to reveal your name written in red ink.

questions.text: Thoth was credited with inventing which system?
questions.correctReaction: I gave humanity its voice in stone and papyrus — hieroglyphic writing was my gift.
questions.wrongReaction: Without my invention, not one word of Egypt's greatness could have been preserved.
questions.trapText: The torches dim and the orange light is replaced by the cold glow of moonlight through an unseen opening.

questions.text: Thoth recorded the verdict of the Weighing of the Heart ceremony.
questions.correctReaction: I stood with my reed and my palette and wrote the outcome of every soul's judgment.
questions.wrongReaction: I was present at every weighing since the ceremony began — my records are complete and unalterable.
questions.trapText: A set of scales appears in the center of the chamber and begins to tip slowly toward one side.

questions.text: I hold the reed and the scroll. My beak is long and curved. I write the fates of souls as they stand before Osiris. Who am I?
questions.correctReaction: You have named me correctly — the record has been updated in your favor.
questions.wrongReaction: I described myself with considerable precision and still you have written the wrong name.
questions.trapText: Your name appears scratched out in the great ledger carved into the chamber wall.

chambers.name: Death Chamber
chambers.introText: I am Anubis. There is no deceiving me, no bargaining, no second chance beyond this threshold. I have guided every soul that has ever died in Egypt through the darkness of the Duat. You are not dead — yet you stand in the chamber where death is the only examiner. Answer truly.
chambers.completeText: Your soul is lighter than the feather. You have spoken only truth in my chamber, and I grant you passage.

questions.text: Anubis was the god of which domain?
questions.correctReaction: I am the one who prepares the body and guides the soul — you have stated it correctly.
questions.wrongReaction: I wrapped the first mummy and I will wrap the last — and still you do not know my purpose.
questions.trapText: Bandages of linen uncoil from the walls and begin to drift through the air of the chamber.

questions.text: Anubis had the head of a jackal.
questions.correctReaction: The black jackal face is mine — it has watched over the dead since before memory.
questions.wrongReaction: You stand before my face and deny what your own eyes see carved into every tomb wall.
questions.trapText: A jackal howl echoes through the sealed stone chamber from somewhere deep below.

questions.text: In the Hall of Two Truths, the heart was weighed against what?
questions.correctReaction: The feather of truth — if your heart is heavier, Ammit devours the soul.
questions.wrongReaction: The scales in the Hall of Two Truths have never held anything other than what I have named.
questions.trapText: A feather appears in mid-air and drifts slowly downward as the floor creaks beneath you.

questions.text: Anubis guided souls through the Duat (underworld) to their final judgment.
questions.correctReaction: Every soul I have ever guided has walked the path I showed them — this is my eternal duty.
questions.wrongReaction: I have walked ten million souls through the Duat and you deny the journey they took.
questions.trapText: The floor becomes transparent and the dark void of the Duat appears beneath your feet.

questions.text: I am the last guardian. Black as the night I rule, my scales weigh your very soul. The desert scavenger wears my face. You cannot deceive me. Who am I?
questions.correctReaction: You have named me — and having named me truthfully, you may pass beyond the final gate.
questions.wrongReaction: You stand in my chamber, before my scales, and still you cannot speak my name.
questions.trapText: The final gate behind you seals with the sound of a tomb being closed for eternity.
